seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Liability Limitations and Exclusions; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Total liability (including under indemnities offered) limited to the lesser of: (a) amount paid under the contract / contract value (subject to liability limit in Research Contract Delegations) OR (b) NZD$500,000. Limited to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct of the University. Consequential or indirect losses, 'hold harmless', and loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill explicitly excluded. | Acceptable: Total liability limit (including indemnities) in line with Research Contract Delegations. Uncapped liability acceptable ONLY for Trusted Funders on contestable rounds. Indirect losses acceptable only if total exposure is capped per Delegations. Carve-outs from cap acceptable for: Fraud, Wilful default, Personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality (only if client revenue < NZD$150M). | Escalation: Refer Research Grants and Contracts Delegations (and Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders on non-contestable rounds).

augmented_flags.clause_title: 1.1 Despite the date of signing, this Agreement shall commence on the Start
augmented_flags.snippet: 1.1 Despite the date of signing, this Agreement shall commence on the Start Date and, unless terminated earlier, shall end on the End Date. The term may be extended for such further period(s) as is/are agreed in writing
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause pertains to variations and extensions, not warranties. The clause allows for extensions to be agreed in writing after the term has expired, which falls outside the UoA Preferred Position for Variations and Extensions (POS-21). The Preferred Position requires all variations to be negotiated and executed in writing prior to the expiry of the current Agreement. This deviation requires escalation.

augmented_flags.snippet: 2.1 Subject to the Subcontractor providing the Research and reports in accordance with this Agreement, the Contractor shall pay the Subcontractor the Fee provided it has received funds from the Funding Agency.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses a payment dependency on the Contractor receiving funds from the Funding Agency, which is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. No specific guidance or template exists for this scenario.

augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses payment terms, specifically the timing of payment and milestone satisfaction, but this topic is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. augmented_flags.clause_title: 6.2 The maximum aggregate liability of the Subcontractor to the Contractor
augmented_flags.snippet: 6.2 The maximum aggregate liability of the Subcontractor to the Contractor relating to this Agreement is limited to an amount equal to the total amount paid by the Contractor to the Subcontractor under this Agreement up to the date of the first event giving rise to liability.

These omissions prevent it from being fully compliant with the preferred tier. The clause is therefore flagged as amber, requiring confirmation against Research Contract Delegations and potential escalation if carve-outs or exclusions are missing.

augmented_flags.snippet: 8.1 Subject to clause 9.1 and any obligations imposed on the Contractor by the Funding Agency, the parties agree that they will each keep secret and confidential the terms of this Agreement and all information of a secret, confidential, or proprietary nature concerning the business or affairs of the other firm and which may come into their knowledge as a result of performance under this Agreement, including the results of the Research.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 17.1 If a dispute arising out of this Agreement occurs between the parties,
augmented_flags.snippet: 17.1 If a dispute arising out of this Agreement occurs between the parties, then the parties will in good faith try to resolve that dispute. If the dispute is not resolved within ten (10) working days from the date the dispute was advised in writing then the dispute will be referred to each party’s nominee, who shall negotiate in order to achieve an acceptable resolution. If no resolution can be reached within a further ten (10) working days then the parties will mediate, using a Mediator appointed by the President of the New Zealand Law Society (“the Mediator”). The Mediation agreement will be final and binding on the parties.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the UoA-sensitive topic of Dispute Resolution and aligns partially with the UoA Preferred Position (POS-18). It includes good faith discussions and mediation, which are consistent with the Preferred Position. However, it does not explicitly mention escalation to senior management before mediation, nor does it clarify that the process must not prevent urgent interlocutory action through courts. Additionally, the clause states that the mediation agreement will be 'final and binding,' which may limit the ability to pursue arbitration or litigation if necessary. These deviations place the clause in the Acceptable tier, but it requires confirmation by the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel.

The clause does not explicitly contradict the acceptable tier but requires confirmation that it aligns with the specific funding agency's requirements. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, or General Counsel is recommended for final review.
